description:
You are a DevOps engineer who has been given three local “API-like”
resources placed under /home/user/mock_api.  
Your goal is to test those resources with curl, extract a few key
fields, and write a single unified log file that will later be
consumed by an automated monitoring dashboard.

Resources that already exist:
1. /home/user/mock_api/inventory.json      (static JSON)
2. /home/user/mock_api/user.json           (static JSON)
3. /home/user/mock_api/health.txt          (plain-text)

IMPORTANT:  
• Interact with the files strictly through curl.  
  – Example pattern (do NOT copy verbatim):  
    curl -s file:///home/user/mock_api/inventory.json  
• Do NOT open the files directly with cat, jq, etc.; those tools must
  receive their data through curl’s stdout pipeline.  
• All work must be done as the normal user (no sudo).

Logging requirements
-------------------------------------------------
Create the directory:
    /home/user/logs
Create the file:
    /home/user/logs/api_debug.log

Write exactly three lines to api_debug.log—one per “endpoint”—using the
precise, machine-parseable format:

TIMESTAMP | ENDPOINT | STATUS_CODE | DETAIL

For this exercise the timestamp must be the literal string: N/A  
(The dashboard’s clock is currently under maintenance.)

Populate the file in the following fixed order and values:

1. N/A | inventory | 200 | total_items=3
2. N/A | user      | 200 | first_user=alice
3. N/A | health    | 200 | message=OK

Formatting rules the grader will check:
• Fields separated by a single space, a single vertical bar “|”, and a
  single space.  
• UNIX line endings (LF).  
• No trailing spaces on any line.  
• The log must contain exactly three lines and nothing else.

Hints (not commands):
• Use jq to extract “.total_items” from inventory.json and
  “.users[0].username” from user.json.  
• Capture curl’s exit information with “-w '%{http_code}'”.  
• Redirect or append the formatted string to the log file.

Complexity expectation
-------------------------------------------------
A correct solution will usually involve 6–8 shell commands:
  – Making /home/user/logs  
  – Three separate curl calls  
  – Two jq extractions  
  – echo/printf statements to construct the log  

When you are done, /home/user/logs/api_debug.log should exist and match
the exact content specified above.
truth:
Directory and file seed state (already present before the agent starts):

/home/user/mock_api/inventory.json
----------------------------------
{
  "total_items": 3,
  "items": ["widget", "gadget", "doodad"]
}

/home/user/mock_api/user.json
-----------------------------
{
  "users": [
    { "id": 1, "username": "alice" },
    { "id": 2, "username": "bob" }
  ]
}

/home/user/mock_api/health.txt
------------------------------
OK


Expected artefact created by the agent:

File: /home/user/logs/api_debug.log  
Permissions: default user-writable (0644 is acceptable)  
Exact byte contents (including newlines):

N/A | inventory | 200 | total_items=3
N/A | user      | 200 | first_user=alice
N/A | health    | 200 | message=OK

(Each line terminated by a single LF, no extra blank line at EOF.)
